seoTitle: Awinic Products - Audio Amplifier, Power Management, RF IC Distributor
seoDescription: Browse Awinic audio amplifiers, power management ICs, RF front-end modules, and LED drivers. LiTong is your authorized distributor for analog and mixed-signal solutions.

faqs.question: What product categories does Awinic offer?
faqs.answer: Awinic offers four main product categories: Audio Amplifiers featuring Class-D and Smart PA technology for smartphone and portable audio applications with high efficiency and superior sound quality. Power Management ICs including battery chargers, DC-DC converters, and LDOs for efficient power delivery in mobile devices. RF Front-End modules comprising RF switches, LNA banks, and antenna tuning solutions for 4G/5G communications. LED Drivers for backlight and flash applications with high efficiency and precise current control. Each category is designed to address specific analog and mixed-signal needs in smartphones, IoT devices, automotive, and consumer electronics markets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the product categories below to find the right Awinic products for your application, or contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Awinic product for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Awinic product requires understanding your application requirements: For audio applications, consider output power requirements, speaker impedance, and desired sound quality features. Smart PAs are recommended for smartphones requiring high output power and advanced protection. For power management, evaluate input voltage range, output current requirements, efficiency needs, and package constraints. For RF applications, consider frequency bands, linearity requirements, and insertion loss specifications. For LED drivers, determine the number of LED channels, current requirements, and dimming control needs. Awinic provides detailed datasheets, application notes, and reference designs to guide selection. Our FAE team can provide personalized recommendations based on your specific system architecture and performance targets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our selection guides in each category or contact LiTong FAE team for personalized product recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between standard Class-D and Smart PA amplifiers?
faqs.answer: Awinic offers both standard Class-D amplifiers and Smart PA (Smart Power Amplifier) solutions with distinct capabilities: Standard Class-D amplifiers provide efficient audio amplification with basic protection features, suitable for applications with straightforward audio requirements and cost constraints. Smart PA amplifiers include advanced features such as adaptive boost converters that maintain constant output power across battery voltage variations, real-time speaker protection algorithms that monitor temperature and excursion, and DSP-based sound enhancement for improved audio quality. Smart PAs also offer higher output power capability, making them ideal for smartphone applications requiring loud, clear sound from small speakers. While standard Class-D amps are suitable for basic applications, Smart PAs are recommended for flagship smartphones and premium audio devices where maximum performance is required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Class-D for cost-sensitive applications. Use Smart PA for high-performance smartphone audio. Contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: Does Awinic offer automotive-qualified products?
faqs.answer: Yes, Awinic offers AEC-Q100 qualified products specifically designed for automotive applications. These automotive-grade ICs undergo rigorous qualification testing including high temperature operating life (HTOL), temperature cycling, electrostatic discharge (ESD) testing, and electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) validation to meet automotive reliability standards. Awinic's automotive product portfolio includes audio amplifiers for infotainment systems with enhanced EMC performance, LED drivers for interior and exterior lighting applications, and power management ICs for various automotive electronic modules. All automotive products are qualified for operation over the -40°C to +125°C temperature range. Awinic continues to expand its automotive offerings to address the growing demand for analog ICs in electric vehicles, ADAS systems, and connected car applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, look for AEC-Q100 qualified Awinic parts. Contact our automotive FAE team for functional safety support and recommendations.

faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for Awinic product selection including: Application engineering assistance from our experienced FAE team who can help with product selection, schematic review, PCB layout optimization, and debugging. Reference designs with complete schematics, BOMs, and layout guidelines for typical applications. Evaluation boards for testing and validation of Awinic solutions. Acoustic tuning support for audio amplifier applications including speaker characterization and sound quality optimization. Software drivers and configuration tools for digital-controlled devices. Technical documentation including datasheets, application notes, and design guides. On-site technical support and training for high-volume projects. We can also coordinate directly with Awinic's factory FAEs for complex technical challenges and provide competitive pricing for production volumes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong technical support team for personalized assistance with Awinic product selection and application design.

categories.name: Audio Amplifiers
categories.description: Awinic audio amplifiers feature advanced Class-D technology and Smart PA solutions designed for smartphone and portable audio applications. These amplifiers deliver high output power with exceptional efficiency, enabling loud, clear sound from compact speakers. The Smart PA series includes integrated boost converters, real-time speaker protection, and DSP-based sound enhancement for premium audio experiences. With output power ranging from 1W to 5W and industry-leading efficiency exceeding 90%, Awinic audio amplifiers are the preferred choice for major smartphone manufacturers.

selectionGuide.title: Awinic Audio Amplifier Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Learn how to select the right Awinic audio amplifier for your application based on power requirements, speaker characteristics, and sound quality needs.

faqs.question: What output power options are available for Awinic audio amplifiers?
faqs.answer: Awinic offers audio amplifiers with output power options ranging from 1W to 5W to address different application requirements. The standard Class-D amplifiers provide 1-2W output power suitable for basic smartphone and tablet applications. The Smart PA series offers higher output power from 3W to 5W, enabling louder sound from micro speakers commonly used in modern smartphones. The output power capability depends on the supply voltage and speaker impedance - Awinic amplifiers can deliver maximum power when operating from a boosted supply voltage provided by the integrated boost converter in Smart PA devices. For applications requiring even higher power, multiple amplifiers can be used in bridge configuration or to drive separate speaker channels. Our FAE team can help calculate the required output power based on your speaker characteristics and SPL targets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select output power based on speaker size and target SPL. Contact our FAE team for power calculation assistance.

faqs.question: How do I choose between Class-D and Smart PA amplifiers?
faqs.answer: Choosing between standard Class-D and Smart PA amplifiers depends on your application requirements and budget: Standard Class-D amplifiers are ideal for cost-sensitive applications with basic audio requirements. They provide good efficiency and acceptable sound quality for mainstream smartphones, tablets, and portable devices. Smart PA amplifiers are recommended for premium applications requiring maximum performance. They include advanced features such as adaptive boost converters that maintain consistent output power as battery voltage drops, real-time speaker protection that prevents damage from over-excursion or overheating, and DSP-based audio enhancement for improved sound quality. Smart PAs also offer higher maximum output power, making them suitable for applications requiring loud, clear sound. While Smart PAs have a higher cost, the improved user experience and speaker protection often justify the investment for flagship products.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Class-D for cost-sensitive applications. Use Smart PA for premium products requiring maximum performance. Contact our FAE team for recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of Awinic Class-D amplifiers?
faqs.answer: Awinic Class-D amplifiers achieve industry-leading efficiency exceeding 90% at typical operating conditions. This high efficiency is achieved through advanced Class-D modulation techniques and optimized output stage design. The efficiency advantage is particularly significant compared to traditional Class-AB amplifiers, which typically achieve only 50-60% efficiency. For battery-powered devices like smartphones, this efficiency translates directly to longer battery life during audio playback. The Smart PA series maintains high efficiency even when operating with the integrated boost converter, thanks to synchronous rectification and advanced power management. Efficiency varies with output power - it is highest at moderate power levels and decreases slightly at very low or maximum output power. Awinic provides detailed efficiency curves in datasheets to help system designers estimate power consumption for their specific use cases.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect >90% efficiency for Awinic Class-D amplifiers. Contact our FAE team for power consumption estimates.

faqs.question: What protection features do Awinic audio amplifiers include?
faqs.answer: Awinic audio amplifiers include comprehensive protection features to ensure reliable operation: Over-current protection prevents damage from output short circuits or excessive load currents. Over-temperature protection monitors die temperature and reduces output power or shuts down the amplifier if safe limits are exceeded. DC offset protection detects and prevents DC voltage at the output that could damage speakers. Under-voltage lockout ensures proper operation by disabling the amplifier when supply voltage is insufficient. The Smart PA series includes additional advanced protection: Real-time speaker temperature estimation using IV-sensing technology protects speakers from thermal damage. Excursion limiting prevents mechanical damage from over-driving small speakers. Adaptive power management optimizes performance while maintaining safe operating margins. These protection features make Awinic amplifiers suitable for demanding applications where reliability is critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: All Awinic amplifiers include comprehensive protection. Smart PA adds advanced speaker protection. Contact our FAE team for protection details.

faqs.question: What package options are available for Awinic audio amplifiers?
faqs.answer: Awinic audio amplifiers are available in various package options to meet different application requirements: WLCSP (Wafer-Level Chip Scale Package) offers the smallest footprint for space-constrained smartphone designs, with packages as small as 1.5mm x 1.5mm. FC-CSP (Flip-Chip CSP) provides improved thermal performance and is commonly used for mid-range to high-end applications. QFN (Quad Flat No-lead) packages offer easier assembly and better accessibility for debugging, suitable for applications where board space is less critical. DFN (Dual Flat No-lead) packages provide a balance of size and ease of use. The package selection depends on available board space, thermal requirements, manufacturing capabilities, and cost considerations. WLCSP packages require more precise assembly processes but enable the most compact designs. QFN packages are recommended for applications where ease of assembly and rework are priorities.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use WLCSP for minimum size, QFN for easier assembly. Contact our FAE team for package recommendations.

faqs.question: What is IV sensing and how does it protect speakers?
faqs.answer: IV sensing is a technology that continuously monitors both the current (I) and voltage (V) at the speaker output to estimate speaker temperature and excursion in real-time. The AW87318 uses this information to implement advanced speaker protection algorithms. By measuring the electrical impedance of the speaker, the amplifier can detect when the voice coil is approaching dangerous temperatures or when the diaphragm is exceeding safe excursion limits. When these limits are approached, the amplifier automatically reduces gain or applies compression to protect the speaker while maintaining audio output. This real-time protection is superior to traditional thermal sensors because it responds much faster and can predict thermal issues before they occur. IV sensing also enables speaker characterization, allowing the amplifier to optimize its performance for specific speaker models.
faqs.decisionGuide: IV sensing provides superior speaker protection for high-power applications. Essential for small speakers in smartphones.

faqs.question: How do I configure the DSP features in AW87318?
faqs.answer: The AW87318 DSP features are configured through the I2C control interface. Awinic provides a comprehensive software driver and configuration tools that simplify the setup process. Key DSP features include: Dynamic Range Compression (DRC) which maintains consistent loudness across different content. Psychoacoustic Bass Boost which enhances perceived bass without increasing physical excursion. Equalization filters for customizing the frequency response. The configuration process involves: Initializing the I2C interface and verifying communication. Loading a preset configuration or custom parameters based on your speaker characteristics. Enabling the desired DSP features through control registers. Fine-tuning parameters during acoustic testing to optimize sound quality. Awinic provides reference configurations for common speaker types, and our FAE team can assist with custom tuning for your specific acoustic system.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Awinic provided software tools for DSP configuration. Contact our FAE team for acoustic tuning support.

faqs.question: What is the adaptive boost converter and why is it important?
faqs.answer: The adaptive boost converter integrated in the AW87318 maintains a consistent output supply voltage for the Class-D amplifier stage, independent of the battery voltage. This is important because lithium battery voltage varies from 4.2V when fully charged to around 3.3V when nearly depleted. Without a boost converter, output power would decrease significantly as the battery discharges. The adaptive boost automatically adjusts its output voltage based on the audio signal level - it provides higher voltage only when needed for peak output, improving efficiency during normal listening levels. The boost converter can provide up to 8.5V output, enabling the 5W output power capability. This adaptive behavior optimizes battery life while maintaining consistent audio performance throughout the battery discharge cycle. The boost converter uses synchronous rectification for high efficiency and includes protection features for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Adaptive boost maintains consistent audio performance across battery voltage range. Essential for high-power applications.

faqs.question: How do I optimize PCB layout for AW87318?
faqs.answer: Optimizing PCB layout for the AW87318 is critical for achieving maximum performance and minimizing EMI. Key layout guidelines include: Place the AW87318 close to the speaker connector to minimize output trace length and radiation. Use wide, short traces for the speaker output connections to minimize resistance and inductance. Implement a solid ground plane under the device with multiple vias to the main ground plane for low impedance. Place decoupling capacitors as close as possible to the supply pins, using a combination of 10uF and 0.1uF capacitors. Keep digital control lines (I2C) away from analog audio paths to prevent noise coupling. Use differential routing for the speaker outputs when possible to reduce common-mode radiation. The exposed pad on the bottom of the package should be connected to ground with multiple vias for thermal dissipation. Awinic provides reference layouts and can review customer designs for optimization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow Awinic layout guidelines for optimal performance. Contact our FAE team for layout review.

faqs.question: What speaker types work best with AW87318?
faqs.answer: The AW87318 is optimized for micro speakers commonly used in smartphones and portable devices. These speakers typically have diameters of 10-20mm, impedances of 4-8 ohms, and power handling of 0.5-1W continuous. The Smart PA technology is particularly beneficial for these small speakers because: The high output voltage swing (up to 8.5V) enables higher SPL from low-impedance speakers. The real-time protection prevents damage to the delicate speaker suspensions. The DSP enhancement can compensate for the limited bass response of small speakers. When selecting a speaker, consider: Impedance - 4-ohm speakers will deliver higher SPL but require more current. 8-ohm speakers are easier to drive but produce lower output. Power handling - ensure the speaker can handle the peak power delivered by the amplifier. Frequency response - speakers with extended high-frequency response will benefit more from the DSP bass boost. Awinic can provide speaker characterization services to optimize the amplifier configuration.
faqs.decisionGuide: AW87318 works best with 4-8 ohm micro speakers. Contact our FAE team for speaker selection guidance.

faqs.question: How does AW87318 compare to competing Smart PA solutions?
faqs.answer: The AW87318 offers competitive advantages compared to other Smart PA solutions in the market: The integrated IV sensing provides more accurate speaker protection than temperature-sensor-based approaches used by some competitors. The DSP integration enables advanced audio features without requiring external DSP chips, reducing BOM cost. The efficiency is industry-leading, with >90% efficiency at typical operating points, comparable to or better than competing solutions. The package size is among the smallest available, enabling compact smartphone designs. Awinic's local support in China provides faster response times for customers in the Asian market. The cost-effectiveness is a key advantage - AW87318 delivers flagship performance at a more competitive price point than premium competitors. For customers seeking a balance of performance, features, and cost, the AW87318 represents an excellent choice. Awinic continues to invest in R&D to maintain competitive leadership in the audio amplifier market.
faqs.decisionGuide: AW87318 offers excellent performance-to-cost ratio. Contact our FAE team for competitive analysis.

faqs.question: What makes AW8737 suitable for cost-sensitive applications?
faqs.answer: The AW8737 is designed specifically for cost-sensitive applications while maintaining excellent audio performance. Key cost-saving features include: Simple application circuit requiring minimal external components - just input capacitors, supply decoupling, and output filtering. No external boost converter needed, reducing BOM cost and board space. Compact WCSP-16 package enables smaller PCB designs. Standard Class-D architecture without complex DSP features that add cost. High efficiency reduces battery and thermal management costs. Despite the cost optimization, the AW8737 delivers excellent audio quality with <0.1% THD+N and high PSRR. The device is pin-compatible with other Awinic amplifiers, allowing easy upgrades if more features are needed later. For high-volume applications, Awinic offers competitive pricing that makes the AW8737 an attractive alternative to more expensive premium solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: AW8737 offers excellent value for cost-sensitive applications. Contact our sales team for volume pricing.

faqs.question: How does the EMI reduction technology work in AW8737?
faqs.answer: The AW8737 incorporates several advanced EMI reduction technologies to minimize electromagnetic interference: Edge rate control slows the switching transitions of the output stage, reducing high-frequency harmonic content while maintaining efficiency. Spread spectrum modulation varies the switching frequency slightly around the nominal value, spreading the EMI energy across a wider frequency band and reducing peak emissions. Optimized output stage design minimizes ringing and overshoot that can cause radiated emissions. Differential output architecture reduces common-mode noise. These techniques work together to keep the AW8737's EMI emissions well below regulatory limits (CISPR 22/32 Class B) without requiring extensive external filtering. For most applications, simple ferrite beads on the output traces are sufficient to meet EMI requirements. The EMI performance is validated in Awinic's reference designs, and layout guidelines are provided to ensure customers achieve similar results.
faqs.decisionGuide: AW8737 includes advanced EMI reduction. Follow layout guidelines for best results. Contact our FAE team for EMI support.

faqs.question: What is the quiescent current of AW8737 and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: The AW8737 features ultra-low quiescent current of approximately 1.5mA when idle with no audio signal. This low quiescent current is important for battery-powered devices because it minimizes power consumption during silent periods or when playing quiet content. In typical smartphone use cases where audio is played intermittently, the quiescent current can significantly impact overall battery life. The AW8737 also features a shutdown mode with current consumption <1uA, allowing the system to completely power down the amplifier when not in use. The device can be quickly enabled via a GPIO control signal when audio playback is needed. For applications requiring always-on audio readiness, the low quiescent current ensures minimal battery drain. The efficiency remains high across the output power range, further optimizing battery life during active playback.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low quiescent current extends battery life. Use shutdown mode for minimum power when not in use.

faqs.question: How do I implement pop and click suppression with AW8737?
faqs.answer: The AW8737 includes integrated pop and click suppression circuitry that eliminates audible transients during power-on, power-off, and mode transitions. This feature works by gradually ramping the output DC bias voltage and audio gain during startup, preventing the sudden voltage steps that cause speaker popping. The suppression is automatic and requires no external components or software control. For optimal performance: Ensure adequate supply decoupling with capacitors close to the device. Allow sufficient time for the internal bias circuits to stabilize before enabling playback. Use the shutdown mode rather than removing power for temporary mute functions. The pop and click suppression is validated across the operating temperature range and supply voltage range. If audible artifacts are still present in your application, check for ground bounce or supply voltage droop during switching, and ensure the speaker connections are secure.
faqs.decisionGuide: Pop/click suppression is automatic. Ensure proper decoupling for best performance.

faqs.question: Can AW8737 drive different speaker impedances?
faqs.answer: Yes, the AW8737 can drive speakers with impedances from 4 ohms to 16 ohms, though it is optimized for 4-ohm and 8-ohm speakers commonly used in portable devices. The output power capability varies with speaker impedance: 4-ohm speakers will deliver the maximum 2W output power. 8-ohm speakers will deliver approximately 1W output power. 16-ohm speakers will deliver approximately 0.5W output power. The amplifier automatically adjusts its operation to maintain stability and efficiency across the impedance range. When driving lower impedance speakers, ensure the power supply can deliver the required current. The protection circuits monitor output current and will limit drive if unsafe conditions are detected. For applications requiring stereo output, two AW8737 devices can be used, one for each channel. The device is not recommended for driving loads below 4 ohms as this may trigger over-current protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: AW8737 supports 4-16 ohm speakers. Use 4-ohm for maximum output power.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for AW8737?
faqs.answer: The AW8737 requires minimal thermal management due to its high efficiency and compact thermal design. At typical operating conditions (0.5W output power), the device dissipates less than 50mW of power as heat. The WCSP package has an exposed thermal pad on the bottom that should be connected to the PCB ground plane with multiple vias for heat dissipation. For most applications, the PCB copper area provides sufficient heatsinking to keep the junction temperature within safe limits. The device includes over-temperature protection that will reduce output power or shut down if the die temperature exceeds safe limits. For high ambient temperature applications or continuous high-power operation, ensure adequate PCB copper area and consider airflow if available. Thermal modeling shows the device can operate at full 2W output power up to 85°C ambient temperature with standard PCB designs. For extreme conditions, contact Awinic for thermal design support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimal thermal management needed. Connect thermal pad to ground plane with vias.

faqs.question: What is spread spectrum modulation and how does it reduce EMI?
faqs.answer: Spread spectrum modulation varies the switching frequency of the Class-D amplifier over a small range, spreading the EMI energy across a wider frequency band rather than concentrating it at a single frequency. This reduces peak EMI emissions, making it easier to meet EMC regulations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use AW87329 for applications requiring low EMI without external filters.

faqs.question: Can AW87329 operate without an output filter?
faqs.answer: Yes, the AW87329 supports filter-less operation for speakers with short wire lengths (<10cm). The amplifier uses advanced modulation techniques to reduce high-frequency switching noise. For longer speaker wires, a simple ferrite bead can be added.
faqs.decisionGuide: Filter-less operation works for short speaker wires. Add ferrite beads for longer wires.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of AW87329?
faqs.answer: The AW87329 achieves up to 90% efficiency at typical operating conditions. High efficiency reduces power dissipation and extends battery life in portable applications. The Class-D architecture is significantly more efficient than Class-AB amplifiers.
faqs.decisionGuide: 90% efficiency makes AW87329 ideal for battery-powered applications.

faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive protection features ensure reliable operation.

faqs.question: What is the typical application for AW87329?
faqs.answer: The AW87329 is ideal for smartphones, tablets, portable speakers, IoT devices, and wearable devices. Its compact size, high efficiency, and low EMI make it suitable for space-constrained battery-powered applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: AW87329 is best for portable consumer electronics requiring audio amplification.

For wearable applications, this translates to minimal impact on battery life during standby periods. The automatic shutdown feature further reduces power when no audio is being played.
faqs.decisionGuide: 0.3mA typical quiescent current makes AW87359 ideal for battery-sensitive wearable applications.

faqs.question: What makes AW87359 suitable for TWS earbuds?
faqs.answer: The AW87359 is ideal for TWS earbuds due to several key features: Ultra-low quiescent current (<0.5mA) preserves battery life in the charging case. Compact WCSP-12 package (1.6mm x 1.6mm) fits space-constrained earbud designs. High efficiency (>92%) minimizes heat generation during extended playback. Fast wake-up time (<10ms) enables seamless audio experience. Low EMI design reduces interference with Bluetooth connectivity. The 1.2W output power is sufficient for small earbud drivers while maintaining clarity at typical listening levels.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compact size, low power, and fast wake-up make AW87359 perfect for TWS applications.

faqs.question: How does automatic shutdown work?
faqs.answer: The AW87359 automatic shutdown feature monitors the audio input signal and automatically enters low-power shutdown mode when no audio is detected for a programmable timeout period (typically 50-200ms). When audio resumes, the amplifier quickly wakes up (<10ms) and begins amplification. This feature is transparent to the user and significantly extends battery life in intermittent audio applications like notifications and voice assistants. The shutdown threshold and timeout are configurable via external resistors or I2C interface (depending on variant).

faqs.question: What is the efficiency advantage of AW87359?
faqs.answer: The AW87359 achieves >92% efficiency at typical operating conditions (0.3W output), which is 2-5% higher than typical Class-D amplifiers. This efficiency advantage comes from: Optimized output stage design with low RDS(on) MOSFETs. Advanced modulation scheme reducing switching losses. Intelligent power management that adapts to output level. For a typical smartwatch with 300mAh battery, this efficiency improvement can extend audio playback time by 15-20% compared to standard Class-D amplifiers.

faqs.question: What layout considerations are important for AW87359?
faqs.answer: Key PCB layout considerations for AW87359: Place decoupling capacitors (10μF + 0.1μF) as close as possible to the supply pins. Use short, wide traces for speaker output to minimize EMI radiation. Implement solid ground plane with multiple vias for thermal dissipation. Keep digital control lines away from analog audio paths. The exposed pad should be connected to ground with at least 4 vias. For TWS applications, place the amplifier away from the antenna to prevent interference. Awinic provides reference layouts optimized for wearable applications.

categories.name: Power Management
categories.description: Awinic power management ICs provide comprehensive solutions for battery charging, voltage regulation, and power distribution in mobile devices. The product portfolio includes linear and switching chargers with high efficiency and fast charging capability, DC-DC buck and boost converters for voltage regulation, and low-dropout linear regulators (LDOs) for noise-sensitive applications. These PMICs feature advanced protection, small packages, and low quiescent current for extended battery life.

selectionGuide.title: Awinic Power Management Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Learn how to select the right Awinic power management IC for your battery charging and voltage regulation needs.

faqs.question: What types of battery chargers does Awinic offer?
faqs.answer: Awinic offers both linear and switching battery charger ICs to address different application requirements. Linear chargers (such as AW3210 series) are simple, low-cost solutions ideal for low-capacity batteries and applications where efficiency is less critical. They operate with minimal external components but dissipate more heat during charging. Switching chargers (such as AW3215 series) use buck or buck-boost topology to achieve higher efficiency, especially important for fast charging and high-capacity batteries. These chargers generate less heat and can deliver higher charging currents from limited input power. Awinic chargers support various battery chemistries including Li-ion, Li-polymer, and LiFePO4. Features available include programmable charge currents, safety timers, temperature monitoring, and USB On-The-Go (OTG) support. The selection between linear and switching depends on battery capacity, available input power, thermal constraints, and cost targets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use linear chargers for low-cost, low-power applications. Use switching chargers for fast charging and high efficiency.

faqs.question: How do I select the right DC-DC converter?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Awinic DC-DC converter requires evaluating several key parameters: Input voltage range - ensure the converter can operate from your input source (battery, USB, etc.). Output voltage - buck converters step down voltage, boost converters step up voltage, buck-boost can do both. Output current capability - select a converter with adequate current rating including margin for peak loads. Efficiency requirements - switching converters offer high efficiency (>90%) but may have more output ripple than LDOs. Switching frequency - higher frequencies allow smaller inductors but may increase EMI. Package size - WLCSP for minimum size, QFN for easier assembly. Special features - some converters include I2C control, power-good indicators, or sequencing capabilities. Awinic offers buck converters for step-down applications, boost converters for step-up, and buck-boost for applications where input voltage may be above or below the output voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate input/output requirements, current needs, and efficiency targets. Contact our FAE team for selection assistance.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of Awinic LDOs?
faqs.answer: Awinic LDOs (Low Dropout Regulators) offer several advantages for voltage regulation in noise-sensitive applications: Low noise - LDOs provide clean output voltage with minimal ripple, essential for sensitive analog circuits like RF and audio. Fast transient response - LDOs respond quickly to load current changes, maintaining stable output voltage. Simple design - LDOs require minimal external components (typically just input and output capacitors). No inductor required - unlike switching converters, LDOs do not require magnetic components, reducing BOM cost and EMI. Low quiescent current - Awinic LDOs feature ultra-low quiescent current for battery-powered applications. Wide output voltage range - available in fixed and adjustable output versions. Awinic offers LDOs with various current capabilities from 50mA to 500mA, with features like enable control, power-good indication, and thermal protection. While less efficient than switching converters for large voltage drops, LDOs are ideal when clean output voltage is priority.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use LDOs for noise-sensitive applications. Use switching converters for high efficiency with large voltage drops.

faqs.question: How does fast charging work with Awinic charger ICs?
faqs.answer: Awinic charger ICs support various fast charging protocols to reduce battery charging time. Fast charging works by increasing the charging current beyond standard USB limits (500mA). Awinic chargers support: High-voltage charging - accepting input voltages up to 12V or higher from dedicated chargers, allowing higher power delivery without excessive current. High-current charging - supporting charging currents up to 5A for large batteries. Protocols supported include USB BC1.2, Quick Charge, and USB Power Delivery (PD). The charger IC communicates with the adapter to negotiate the optimal voltage and current. Safety features monitor battery temperature and voltage to prevent overcharging. Fast charging typically charges the battery to 70-80% quickly, then switches to standard charging for the remaining capacity to preserve battery life. Awinic chargers include comprehensive protection for safe fast charging operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select charger based on required charging speed and supported protocols. Contact our FAE team for fast charging design support.

faqs.question: What protection features do Awinic PMICs include?
faqs.answer: Awinic power management ICs include comprehensive protection features for safe and reliable operation: Over-voltage protection (OVP) prevents damage from excessive input voltage. Under-voltage lockout (UVLO) disables operation when input voltage is insufficient. Over-current protection (OCP) limits output current to safe levels. Short-circuit protection (SCP) responds to output short circuits. Over-temperature protection (OTP) monitors die temperature and reduces power or shuts down if limits are exceeded. Battery protection features include over-voltage, under-voltage, and temperature monitoring during charging. Some devices also include reverse current protection and power-on sequencing. These protection features are essential for preventing damage to the PMIC, battery, and system components. Awinic PMICs are designed to fail-safe, protecting the system under all fault conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: All Awinic PMICs include comprehensive protection. Review datasheets for specific protection thresholds.

The I2C interface enables flexible configuration of charging parameters and real-time monitoring of charging status.
features: Synchronous buck topology for high efficiency
Support for USB BC1.2 and Quick Charge

faeReview.author: James Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The AW3215 is my recommended solution for applications requiring fast charging capability. The synchronous buck topology delivers excellent efficiency - I typically see >92% at 2A charging current, which means minimal heat generation even during fast charging. The I2C interface is well-implemented with clear register definitions that make software integration straightforward. I particularly appreciate the comprehensive safety features - the device monitors battery temperature and will automatically reduce charge current or suspend charging if safety limits are exceeded. The USB OTG support is useful for applications that need to power external USB devices. For high-volume smartphone designs, the AW3215 offers an excellent balance of performance, features, and cost. The QFN package is easy to assemble and provides good thermal performance.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent fast charging solution with high efficiency and comprehensive safety features

faqs.question: What charging protocols does AW3215 support?
faqs.answer: The AW3215 supports multiple charging protocols to enable fast charging from various power sources: USB BC1.2 (Battery Charging Specification 1.2) for standard USB charging up to 1.5A. Quick Charge 2.0/3.0 for high-voltage charging from compatible adapters. USB Power Delivery (PD) for negotiation of optimal voltage and current. The device automatically detects the connected adapter type and configures the optimal charging parameters. For dedicated chargers, the AW3215 can accept input voltages up to 13.5V, enabling high-power charging without excessive current. The I2C interface allows software to override automatic detection and manually configure charging parameters when needed. The charger includes a D+/D- detection circuit for adapter type identification and supports USB On-The-Go (OTG) mode for powering external devices from the battery.
faqs.decisionGuide: AW3215 supports major fast charging protocols. Compatible with most USB adapters and dedicated chargers.

faqs.question: How do I configure charging parameters via I2C?
faqs.answer: The AW3215 charging parameters are configured through the I2C interface by writing to specific control registers. Key configurable parameters include: Charge current - programmable from 100mA to 3A in steps. Charge voltage - programmable to match your battery chemistry (typically 4.2V for Li-ion). Safety timer - configurable timeout for charge termination. Input current limit - sets maximum current drawn from the input source. The configuration process involves: Initializing the I2C interface and verifying communication by reading the device ID register. Writing to the charge control register to set charge current and voltage. Enabling the charger and monitoring status registers for charging progress. Reading fault registers to detect any issues during charging. Awinic provides a software driver with API functions for common operations. The I2C interface supports standard (100kHz) and fast (400kHz) modes. Our FAE team can provide example code and configuration guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Awinic provided software drivers for I2C configuration. Contact our FAE team for programming support.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of AW3215 during charging?
faqs.answer: The AW3215 achieves excellent efficiency due to its synchronous buck topology. Typical efficiency is >90% at 2A charging current from a 5V input, and >85% at 3A maximum current. The efficiency varies with input voltage, output current, and battery voltage: Higher input voltage relative to battery voltage improves efficiency. Mid-range charge currents (1-2A) typically show peak efficiency. As the battery approaches full charge, efficiency decreases because the voltage differential reduces. The synchronous rectification (using a low-side MOSFET instead of a diode) significantly improves efficiency compared to non-synchronous designs. This high efficiency means less heat generation, allowing sustained fast charging without thermal throttling. For a typical smartphone charging scenario (3.7V battery, 5V input, 2A current), the AW3215 dissipates less than 1W of power as heat, enabling compact designs without extensive thermal management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect >90% efficiency at typical operating points. High efficiency enables compact fast charging designs.

faqs.question: How does the battery protection work in AW3215?
faqs.answer: The AW3215 includes comprehensive battery protection features to ensure safe charging: Over-voltage protection (OVP) monitors battery voltage and stops charging if it exceeds the programmed threshold (typically 4.25V). Under-voltage protection prevents charging of deeply discharged batteries until they reach a safe voltage. Over-current protection limits charge current to programmed levels and responds to fault conditions. Temperature protection uses an external NTC thermistor to monitor battery temperature, suspending charging if temperature exceeds safe limits (typically 0°C to 45°C for charging). Safety timer terminates charging if the process takes longer than expected, preventing indefinite charging of faulty batteries. Short-circuit protection detects output shorts and limits current. These protections work together to prevent battery damage, thermal runaway, and safety hazards. All protection events are reported via status registers accessible through the I2C interface.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive protection is automatic. Connect NTC thermistor for temperature protection.

faqs.question: What external components are required for AW3215?
faqs.answer: The AW3215 requires minimal external components for a complete charging solution: Input capacitor - 10uF ceramic capacitor close to the input pins for filtering and stability. Output capacitor - 10uF ceramic capacitor at the battery connection for output filtering. Inductor - 2.2uH power inductor rated for peak charging current. Sense resistor - current sense resistor for charge current programming and monitoring. NTC thermistor - 10kohm NTC for battery temperature monitoring (optional but recommended). Pull-up resistors - 4.7kohm resistors for I2C lines. The inductor selection is critical - choose an inductor with saturation current rating above the maximum charge current and low DC resistance for best efficiency. The input capacitor should be placed as close as possible to the IC pins. Awinic provides a reference design with recommended component values and part numbers. The total BOM cost is competitive due to the high integration of the AW3215.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimal external components needed. Use recommended values from reference design.

faqs.question: Can AW3215 support USB OTG mode?
faqs.answer: Yes, the AW3215 supports USB On-The-Go (OTG) mode, allowing the device to power external USB accessories from the battery. In OTG mode, the AW3215 operates in boost configuration, stepping up the battery voltage (typically 3.0V-4.2V) to 5V at the USB connector. The OTG output can deliver up to 1A current to power external devices like USB drives, keyboards, or other accessories. The mode is enabled via the I2C interface by setting the OTG control bit. When OTG is enabled, charging is suspended and the device switches to boost mode. Protection features in OTG mode include: Output over-current protection to prevent damage from excessive load. Output short-circuit protection. Input under-voltage protection to prevent deep battery discharge. The OTG function is useful for smartphones and tablets that need to host USB devices. The boost converter uses synchronous rectification for high efficiency, minimizing battery drain during OTG operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: OTG mode enables USB host functionality. Enable via I2C when needed.

products.shortDescription: Awinic AW3605 2A synchronous buck converter with high efficiency and small solution size.
descriptionParagraphs: The AW3605 is a high-efficiency synchronous buck converter delivering up to 2A output current with minimal external components.
This device features 1.5MHz switching frequency enabling small inductor size and compact PCB layout.
The AW3605 includes comprehensive protection features and is ideal for powering processors, memory, and I/O in portable devices.

faeReview.author: Robert Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The AW3605 is an excellent general-purpose buck converter that I frequently recommend for portable applications. The efficiency is outstanding - I've measured >95% at 1A load from a 3.7V input, which is among the best in its class. The 1.5MHz switching frequency is a sweet spot - it allows small inductor size while keeping switching losses reasonable. The internal compensation simplifies design - you don't need to calculate external compensation components. I particularly like the 100% duty cycle capability which allows the converter to maintain regulation even when the input voltage drops close to the output voltage, extending battery life. The SOT-23-5 package is easy to assemble and provides adequate thermal performance for the 2A rating. For cost-sensitive applications requiring good efficiency and small size, the AW3605 is hard to beat.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent efficiency and small solution size for portable applications

companionParts.description: Battery charger for complete power management

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of AW3605 at different load conditions?
faqs.answer: The AW3605 achieves excellent efficiency across a wide load range due to its synchronous rectification architecture: At light loads (10-50mA), efficiency is typically 85-90%, which is important for extending battery life in standby modes. At medium loads (100-500mA), efficiency peaks at >95%, making it ideal for typical operating conditions. At heavy loads (1-2A), efficiency remains above 90%, minimizing heat generation during peak operation. The efficiency varies with input-output voltage differential - the converter is most efficient when the input voltage is close to the output voltage. The 100% duty cycle capability allows the converter to maintain high efficiency even as the battery voltage drops near the output voltage. For a typical smartphone application with 3.3V output from a 3.7V lithium battery, the AW3605 maintains >93% efficiency across most operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent efficiency across load range. Ideal for battery-powered applications.

faqs.question: How do I select the inductor for AW3605?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right inductor for the AW3605 is important for optimal performance: Inductance value - 2.2uH is recommended for the 1.5MHz switching frequency. This value provides a good balance of ripple current and transient response. Current rating - select an inductor with saturation current rating at least 1.5x the maximum output current (3A for 2A output). DC resistance - lower DCR improves efficiency, especially at high currents. Look for inductors with DCR <100mohm. Shielding - shielded inductors are recommended to minimize EMI radiation. Size - chip inductors (2.0mm x 1.6mm or 2.0mm x 1.2mm) are suitable for compact designs. Core material - ferrite core inductors are preferred for their low loss at switching frequencies. Awinic provides a list of recommended inductors from various manufacturers. The inductor should be placed close to the IC with short, wide traces to minimize parasitic resistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 2.2uH inductor with 3A+ saturation current. Contact our FAE team for recommended parts.

faqs.question: What is the output voltage ripple of AW3605?
faqs.answer: The AW3605 output voltage ripple depends on operating conditions and external components: Typical ripple is 10-30mV peak-to-peak at 1A load with standard 10uF output capacitor. The ripple increases with higher load current and decreases with larger output capacitance. The 1.5MHz switching frequency helps keep ripple low while using small capacitors. For applications requiring ultra-low ripple (such as RF or analog circuits), additional output filtering can be added: A small LC filter (1uH inductor + 10uF capacitor) can reduce ripple to <5mV. A ferrite bead in series with the output provides high-frequency filtering. Increasing output capacitance to 22uF or 47uF reduces ripple proportionally. The AW3605 uses voltage mode control with internal compensation, providing stable operation with various output capacitor types including ceramic and tantalum. For most digital loads, the standard 10uF ceramic output capacitor provides adequate ripple performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard configuration provides 10-30mV ripple. Add filtering for noise-sensitive applications.

faqs.question: How does the soft-start feature work in AW3605?
faqs.answer: The AW3605 includes an internal soft-start circuit that limits inrush current during startup. When the converter is enabled, the output voltage ramps up gradually rather than immediately reaching the regulated value. This soft-start process takes approximately 1ms from enable to full output voltage. The benefits of soft-start include: Reduced inrush current - prevents excessive current draw from the input source during startup, which could cause voltage droop and system reset. Controlled startup - minimizes stress on input capacitors and power switches. Sequential startup - multiple converters can be sequenced by enabling them at different times. The soft-start is automatic and requires no external components. If a fault occurs during startup (such as a short circuit), the converter will detect the fault and shut down before reaching full operation. The soft-start feature is particularly important for battery-powered systems where input current must be carefully managed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Soft-start is automatic. No external components needed for startup control.

faqs.question: Can AW3605 operate at 100% duty cycle?
faqs.answer: Yes, the AW3605 can operate at 100% duty cycle, which means the high-side switch remains continuously on and the converter operates in dropout mode. This is important for battery-powered applications because: As the battery discharges, its voltage approaches the regulated output voltage. Without 100% duty cycle capability, the converter would lose regulation when input voltage drops below the required headroom. With 100% duty cycle, the converter passes the input voltage directly to the output (minus small resistive drops), maintaining regulation even with minimal voltage differential. This extends battery life by allowing the battery to discharge to lower voltages while still powering the system. The dropout voltage is determined by the high-side switch resistance (typically 100-150mohm) multiplied by the load current. At 2A load, dropout voltage is approximately 200-300mV. The converter automatically transitions to 100% duty cycle when needed, with no external control required.
faqs.decisionGuide: 100% duty cycle extends battery life. Automatic operation, no configuration needed.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for AW3605?
faqs.answer: The AW3605 requires minimal thermal management due to its high efficiency and compact thermal design. At typical operating conditions (1A output, 3.3V output from 3.7V input), the device dissipates less than 50mW of power. The SOT-23-5 package has thermal resistance of approximately 200°C/W junction-to-ambient. With 50mW dissipation, temperature rise is only 10°C above ambient. For maximum load (2A) and worst-case conditions, dissipation may reach 200-300mW, resulting in 40-60°C temperature rise. The device includes over-temperature protection that shuts down if the junction temperature exceeds safe limits. For most applications, no special thermal management is required beyond standard PCB design practices: Use adequate copper area for the ground connection. Connect the GND pin to a solid ground plane with multiple vias. Keep other heat-generating components away from the converter. For high ambient temperature applications (>85°C), additional copper area or airflow may be beneficial.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimal thermal management needed. Follow standard PCB practices.

The integrated battery temperature monitoring provides an extra layer of safety, suspending charging if the battery gets too hot or cold. I appreciate the simple I2C interface for configuration and monitoring. The QFN-16 package offers good thermal performance while maintaining a compact footprint. Overall, a reliable and cost-effective charging solution.

faqs.question: What is power path management?
faqs.answer: Power path management is a feature that allows the system to operate directly from the input power source while simultaneously charging the battery. Without power path management, the system must draw power from the battery even when external power is available, causing unnecessary charge cycles. The AW3215's power path management: Prioritizes system power from the adapter when available. Charges the battery with remaining available current. Allows instant system startup even with deeply discharged battery. Prevents battery drain when adapter is connected. This extends battery life by reducing charge/discharge cycles and ensures continuous system operation.

faqs.question: How fast can AW3215 charge a battery?
faqs.answer: The AW3215 supports charging currents up to 2A, enabling fast charging of single-cell Li-Ion batteries. Charging time depends on battery capacity: A 2000mAh battery can be charged from 0% to 80% in approximately 1 hour at 2A. Full charge takes approximately 1.5-2 hours including the slower CV phase. The actual charging current is automatically adjusted based on: Input power availability (USB vs adapter). Battery temperature and condition. System power consumption. The charger automatically transitions through trickle charge (for deeply discharged batteries), constant current (main charging phase), and constant voltage (topping off) phases for safe and efficient charging.

faqs.question: What safety features does AW3215 include?
faqs.answer: The AW3215 includes comprehensive safety features for reliable battery charging: Battery temperature monitoring with automatic charge suspension outside safe range (typically 0°C to 50°C). Over-voltage protection prevents damage from faulty adapters. Over-current protection limits charging current to safe levels. Thermal regulation reduces charging current if die temperature exceeds safe limits. Timer protection prevents indefinite charging of faulty batteries. Reverse leakage protection prevents battery discharge when input is removed. These protections ensure safe charging under various fault conditions and comply with battery safety standards.

faqs.question: How do I configure charging parameters?
faqs.answer: The AW3215 charging parameters can be configured through the I2C interface: Charging current can be set from 100mA to 2A in 100mA steps. Input current limit can be set to match adapter capability (500mA, 1A, 1.5A, 2A, etc.). Charge voltage is factory-set to 4.2V but can be adjusted for different battery chemistries. Safety timers can be configured for different battery capacities. Temperature thresholds can be adjusted for specific applications. Awinic provides a software driver and configuration tools. Default settings are suitable for most standard Li-Ion batteries. Contact our FAE team for custom configuration guidance.

faqs.question: What is the charging efficiency?
faqs.answer: The AW3215 achieves >90% charging efficiency at typical operating conditions (1A charging from 5V input). This high efficiency is achieved through synchronous switching topology with optimized power MOSFETs. Efficiency benefits: Less heat generation compared to linear chargers. Reduced power consumption from adapter. Faster charging with less thermal throttling. Ability to charge at higher currents within thermal constraints. Efficiency varies with input voltage and charging current - highest efficiency is achieved at moderate currents (0.5-1.5A) with input voltage close to battery voltage.

products.name: Ultra-Low Noise LDO Regulator
products.shortDescription: Awinic AW3710 ultra-low noise LDO for RF and analog circuits with high PSRR and fast transient response.

This device delivers up to 300mA output current with exceptional noise performance of only 20μVRMS and high PSRR of 75dB at 1kHz.
The fast transient response ensures stable voltage during sudden load changes, critical for RF power amplifiers and digital circuits.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Analog Design
faeReview.content: The AW3710 is my go-to LDO for noise-sensitive applications. The 20μVRMS noise figure is exceptional - I've measured it at 18μV in typical conditions, which is better than many premium competitors. The high PSRR is particularly valuable for RF applications where switching noise from buck converters can cause interference. I've used this LDO successfully for camera sensor power, audio codec supply, and RF PA bias. The fast transient response prevents voltage droop during sudden current demands, which is critical for RF power amplifiers that draw pulsed current. The small WLCSP-4 package is perfect for space-constrained smartphone designs. I recommend placing a 1μF ceramic capacitor close to the output for best noise performance. Overall, an excellent low-noise LDO at a competitive price point.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-low noise and high PSRR ideal for RF and analog applications

faqs.question: How low is the output noise of AW3710?
faqs.answer: The AW3710 achieves ultra-low output noise of 20μVRMS (typical) measured over 10Hz to 100kHz bandwidth. This is among the lowest noise levels for LDOs in its class. The low noise is achieved through: Careful bandgap reference design with filtering. Internal noise reduction circuitry. Optimized error amplifier design. For comparison, typical general-purpose LDOs have 50-100μV noise, while premium ultra-low noise LDOs can achieve <10μV. The AW3710 strikes an excellent balance between noise performance and cost. For even lower noise, additional output filtering can be added.

faqs.question: What is PSRR and why is it important?
faqs.answer: PSRR (Power Supply Rejection Ratio) measures how well the LDO rejects noise and ripple from the input supply. The AW3710 achieves 75dB PSRR at 1kHz, meaning it attenuates input ripple by a factor of 5600x (75dB = 20*log(5600)). High PSRR is important when: The input supply comes from a noisy buck converter. Powering RF circuits where switching noise can cause interference. Supplying analog sensors where ripple affects measurement accuracy. Driving audio circuits where power supply noise becomes audible. The AW3710 maintains high PSRR across a wide frequency range, providing effective noise rejection for most applications.

faqs.question: What capacitors should I use with AW3710?
faqs.answer: The AW3710 is stable with small ceramic capacitors, simplifying BOM and reducing size: Input capacitor: 1μF ceramic for high-frequency bypassing. Output capacitor: 1μF ceramic minimum, 2.2-4.7μF recommended for best transient response. Capacitor type: X5R or X7R ceramic with low ESR. Placement: Place capacitors as close as possible to the pins. For lowest noise performance: Add a 10nF ceramic capacitor in parallel with the main output capacitor. Use a small ferrite bead in series with the input for high-frequency filtering. These recommendations ensure stable operation with excellent noise performance.

faqs.question: How do I select the output voltage?
faqs.answer: The AW3710 is available in fixed output voltage versions (1.2V, 1.8V, 2.5V, 2.8V, 3.0V, 3.3V) and adjustable versions. Fixed voltage versions: Simplest implementation - no external resistors needed. Factory-trimmed for accuracy. Recommended for standard voltages. Adjustable versions: Set voltage with external resistor divider. Formula: Vout = 0.5V × (1 + R1/R2). Allows custom voltages and future adjustment. Select output voltage based on: Load circuit requirements (check datasheet for recommended supply). Input voltage availability (must have adequate headroom). Power consumption considerations (lower voltage reduces power).

faqs.question: What is the dropout voltage?
faqs.answer: Dropout voltage is the minimum input-to-output voltage differential required for the LDO to maintain regulation. The AW3710 has low dropout voltage of 150mV at 300mA load, meaning: For 3.3V output, input must be at least 3.45V. For 1.8V output, input must be at least 1.95V. Low dropout is important for: Battery-powered applications where input voltage drops as battery discharges. Efficient operation with minimal power loss in the LDO. Extending battery life by utilizing more of the battery capacity. The dropout voltage increases with load current - at light loads, dropout may be only 50-100mV.

products.name: Multi-Channel PMIC for Wearables

descriptionParagraphs: The AW3320 is a highly integrated power management IC (PMIC) designed specifically for wearable devices such as smartwatches and fitness trackers.
This device integrates two high-efficiency buck converters and three LDO regulators in a compact package, providing complete system power from a single Li-Ion battery.
The intelligent power sequencing and low quiescent current maximize battery life in always-on wearable applications.

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: FAE - Wearable Electronics
faeReview.content: The AW3320 is an excellent PMIC solution for wearables that I've used in several smartwatch projects. The integration of multiple power rails in a single chip significantly reduces BOM count and board space - replacing what would typically require 5 separate ICs. The power sequencing is particularly well-designed for wearable SoCs, ensuring proper startup and shutdown sequences. The quiescent current of <50μA is critical for wearable battery life - I've measured it at 35μA in sleep mode. The I2C interface allows dynamic voltage scaling to optimize power consumption based on activity level. The two buck converters efficiently power the processor and display, while the three LDOs provide clean power for sensors, audio, and RF. I recommend this PMIC for any wearable design requiring multiple power rails with minimal board space.

faqs.question: What is a PMIC and why use one?
faqs.answer: PMIC (Power Management Integrated Circuit) combines multiple power regulation functions in a single chip. Benefits of using AW3320 PMIC: Reduces component count - replaces 5+ discrete ICs. Saves board space - single QFN-24 vs multiple packages. Simplifies design - integrated sequencing and control. Optimized for wearables - low quiescent current, compact size. Cost reduction - lower total BOM cost than discrete solution. The AW3320 specifically integrates 2 buck converters and 3 LDOs, covering typical smartwatch power requirements: Buck1: Processor core (1.0-1.2V). Buck2: Display and peripherals (1.8-3.3V). LDO1: RF transceiver (1.8V). LDO2: Audio codec (1.8V). LDO3: Sensors (2.8V).

faqs.question: How low is the quiescent current?
faqs.answer: The AW3320 achieves ultra-low quiescent current of <50μA with all channels enabled in sleep mode. This is critical for wearable battery life. Current breakdown: Buck converters: ~15μA each in PFM mode. LDO regulators: ~5μA each. Control circuitry: ~10μA. Total: 35-50μA depending on configuration. For a typical 300mAh smartwatch battery, this quiescent current allows months of standby time. The device supports multiple power modes: Active mode: Full performance with higher quiescent current. Sleep mode: Reduced quiescent with maintained regulation. Deep sleep: Ultra-low power with wake-on-interrupt.

faqs.question: How do I configure output voltages?
faqs.answer: The AW3320 output voltages are configurable via I2C interface: Buck converters: Programmable from 0.6V to 3.4V in 10mV steps. LDO regulators: Programmable from 1.0V to 3.3V in 50mV steps. Configuration options: Factory default settings for typical applications. Runtime adjustment via I2C for dynamic voltage scaling. OTP (One-Time Programmable) for custom default voltages. Power sequencing order and delays are also configurable. This flexibility allows optimization for different processor and peripheral requirements. Awinic provides software drivers and reference configurations for popular wearable platforms.

faqs.question: What is power sequencing and why is it important?
faqs.answer: Power sequencing is the controlled order in which power rails are turned on and off. Proper sequencing is critical for: Preventing latch-up in CMOS circuits. Ensuring proper reset and initialization of processors. Avoiding bus contention during power transitions. Protecting sensitive analog circuits. The AW3320 provides programmable power sequencing: Configurable turn-on order for all 5 channels. Adjustable delays between channel enable. Power-good monitoring with interrupt capability. Automatic controlled shutdown sequence. Typical sequence for smartwatch: 1. LDO3 (sensors) - 2ms delay - 2. LDO2 (audio) - 2ms delay - 3. LDO1 (RF) - 5ms delay - 4. Buck2 (peripherals) - 2ms delay - 5. Buck1 (processor).

faqs.question: How do I monitor power status?
faqs.answer: The AW3320 provides comprehensive power monitoring through I2C: Real-time voltage monitoring for all 5 channels. Current monitoring on buck converters (optional). Temperature monitoring with overtemperature alarm. Power-good status for each channel. Fault detection and reporting. Interrupt generation for fault conditions. This monitoring capability enables: Proactive power management and optimization. Fault detection and graceful degradation. Battery life estimation and low-battery warnings. System debugging and diagnostics. The I2C interface operates at standard (100kHz) and fast (400kHz) modes. Awinic provides Linux and RTOS drivers for easy integration.

categories.name: RF Front-End
categories.description: Awinic RF front-end solutions enable high-performance wireless connectivity for 4G/5G smartphones and IoT devices. The product portfolio includes RF switches for antenna and band selection, LNA (Low Noise Amplifier) banks for receiver sensitivity enhancement, and antenna tuning solutions for optimizing RF performance across multiple frequency bands. These products are characterized by low insertion loss, high linearity, and excellent isolation to meet the demanding requirements of carrier aggregation and MIMO applications.

selectionGuide.title: Awinic RF Front-End Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Learn how to select the right Awinic RF switches and LNAs for your wireless communication needs.

faqs.question: What types of RF switches does Awinic offer?
faqs.answer: Awinic offers a comprehensive portfolio of RF switches for various applications: SPST (Single Pole Single Throw) switches for simple on/off control of RF paths. SPDT (Single Pole Double Throw) switches for antenna switching or band selection. SP3T/SP4T (Single Pole 3/4 Throw) switches for multi-band or multi-antenna systems. DPDT (Double Pole Double Throw) switches for differential signal routing. These switches cover frequency ranges from DC to 6GHz, supporting 4G LTE and 5G NR applications. Key specifications include low insertion loss (typically <0.5dB), high isolation (>30dB), and excellent linearity (IIP3 >60dBm) to meet carrier aggregation requirements. The switches are available in compact packages including WLCSP and QFN for space-constrained smartphone designs. Awinic also offers switches with integrated GPIO or MIPI RFFE control interfaces for flexible system integration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select switch type based on routing requirements. Contact our RF FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right RF switch for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Awinic RF switch requires evaluating several key parameters: Frequency range - ensure the switch supports all frequency bands in your application (typically 600MHz-6GHz for 5G). Insertion loss - lower loss improves receiver sensitivity and transmitter efficiency. Awinic switches offer <0.5dB insertion loss. Isolation - higher isolation prevents signal leakage between ports. >30dB is typical for good performance. Linearity (IIP3) - important for carrier aggregation to prevent intermodulation distortion. >60dBm is recommended for 5G applications. Power handling - ensure the switch can handle maximum transmit power (typically >30dBm for cellular). Control interface - GPIO for simple control, MIPI RFFE for smartphone platforms. Package size - WLCSP for minimum size, QFN for easier assembly. Power consumption - consider control voltage and current requirements. Our RF FAE team can provide detailed recommendations based on your specific frequency plan and system architecture.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate frequency range, loss, isolation, and linearity requirements. Contact our RF FAE team for assistance.

faqs.question: What are LNA banks and when are they needed?
faqs.answer: LNA (Low Noise Amplifier) banks are multi-channel, multi-band LNAs that provide gain and noise figure improvement for receiver applications. They are needed when: Operating in frequency bands with weak received signals where receiver sensitivity is critical. Supporting carrier aggregation where multiple bands are received simultaneously. Implementing diversity reception with multiple antennas for improved signal quality. Awinic LNA banks provide: Low noise figure (typically <1dB) to improve receiver sensitivity. High gain (15-20dB) to overcome mixer and filter losses. Excellent linearity to handle strong interfering signals. Multi-band coverage in a single device, reducing BOM count. Integrated bypass mode for high-input-signal conditions. The LNA is placed early in the receive chain to amplify weak signals while adding minimal noise. This improves the overall system noise figure and extends communication range. LNA banks are essential for 5G smartphones and high-performance IoT devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use LNA banks when receiver sensitivity is critical. Essential for 5G and carrier aggregation.

faqs.question: What is antenna tuning and why is it important?
faqs.answer: Antenna tuning is the process of optimizing the impedance match between the antenna and the RF front-end to maximize power transfer and efficiency. It is important because: Smartphones must operate across multiple frequency bands (700MHz-6GHz) with a limited number of antennas. Antenna impedance varies with frequency, usage mode (handheld vs table), and environmental conditions. Without tuning, impedance mismatch causes signal reflection, reducing radiated power and receiver sensitivity. Awinic antenna tuning solutions include: Tunable capacitors that adjust antenna resonance dynamically. Impedance matching networks optimized for different bands. Control algorithms that select optimal tuning states. Benefits include: Improved TRP (Total Radiated Power) and TIS (Total Isotropic Sensitivity). Support for more bands with fewer antennas. Compensation for detuning caused by hand effects or nearby objects. Extended battery life due to improved efficiency. Antenna tuning is essential for modern smartphones supporting global LTE and 5G bands.
faqs.decisionGuide: Antenna tuning improves performance across multiple bands. Essential for 5G smartphones.

faqs.question: How do Awinic RF products support 5G applications?
faqs.answer: Awinic RF front-end products are designed to meet the demanding requirements of 5G New Radio (NR) applications: Extended frequency coverage - Awinic switches and LNAs support frequencies up to 6GHz, covering sub-6GHz 5G bands. High linearity - IIP3 >65dBm supports carrier aggregation with multiple component carriers. Low insertion loss - <0.4dB at 3.5GHz preserves link budget for high-frequency bands. Excellent isolation - >35dB prevents interference between bands in carrier aggregation. Fast switching - <1us switching time enables dynamic spectrum sharing. MIPI RFFE control - compatible with modern smartphone platform interfaces. These characteristics enable: 5G NSA (Non-Standalone) and SA (Standalone) operation. 4G/5G dual connectivity. Massive MIMO with multiple antenna paths. Beamforming with phased antenna arrays. Awinic continues to develop new RF products to address emerging 5G requirements including mmWave frequencies and wider channel bandwidths.
faqs.decisionGuide: Awinic RF products support sub-6GHz 5G with excellent performance. Contact our RF FAE for 5G design support.
